differentials.name: PREVERTEBRAL SEGMENT (V1) ascends posterosuperiorly between longus colli + anterior scalene muscle; enters transverse foramen at C6
differentials.raw: PREVERTEBRAL SEGMENT (V1) ascends posterosuperiorly between longus colli + anterior scalene muscle; enters transverse foramen at C6

differentials.name: MIDCERVICAL SEGMENT (V2) ascends through transverse foramina of C6 to C2 in close proximity to uncinate processes [Anterior meningeal a.]
differentials.raw: MIDCERVICAL SEGMENT (V2) ascends through transverse foramina of C6 to C2 in close proximity to uncinate processes

differentials.name: ATLANTIC SEGMENT = Atlas loop (V3) exits transverse foramen of atlas; passes posteriorly in a groove on superior surface of posterior arch of atlas; pierces atlanto-occipital membrane + dura mater to enter cranial cavity
differentials.raw: ATLANTIC SEGMENT = Atlas loop (V3) exits transverse foramen of atlas; passes posteriorly in a groove on superior surface of posterior arch of atlas; pierces atlanto-occipital membrane + dura mater to enter cranial cavity

differentials.name: Posterior meningeal branch to posterior falx + tentorium
differentials.raw: Posterior meningeal branch to posterior falx + tentorium

differentials.name: INTRACRANIAL SEGMENT (V4) ascends anteriorly + laterally around medulla to reach midline at pontomedullary junction;
differentials.raw: INTRACRANIAL SEGMENT (V4) ascends anteriorly + laterally around medulla to reach midline at pontomedullary junction;

differentials.name: Internal auditory a. [Superior cerebellar a.] [Posterior cerebral a.]
Medullary + pontine perforating branches ◊  May terminate in common AICA-PICA trunk
differentials.raw: Medullary + pontine perforating branches ◊  May terminate in common AICA-PICA trunk

differentials.name: Premedullar segment = caudal loop around medulla, may descend below level of foramen magnum
differentials.raw: Premedullar segment = caudal loop around medulla, may descend below level of foramen magnum

differentials.name: Retromedullar segment = ascending portion up to the level of 4th ventricle and tonsils
differentials.raw: Retromedullar segment = ascending portion up to the level of 4th ventricle and tonsils

differentials.name: Supratonsillar segment = the most cranial point is the choroidal point
differentials.raw: Supratonsillar segment = the most cranial point is the choroidal point

differentials.name: perpendicular line from choroid point onto Twining's line = TTT-line (Twining's
differentials.raw: perpendicular line from choroid point onto Twining's line = TTT-line (Twining's

differentials.name: perpendicular line from choroid point cuts CT-line (Clivus-Torcular line) < 1 mm
differentials.raw: perpendicular line from choroid point cuts CT-line (Clivus-Torcular line) < 1 mm

context: originates from subclavian a. proximal to thyrocervical trunk; left vertebral a. usually greater than right cerebral a.; left vertebral a. may originate directly from aorta (5%) Branches:   muscular branches Branches: Branches: anastomoses with contralateral side to form basilar artery at clivus Branches: Anterior Inferior Cerebellar Artery =  AICA = first branch of basilar artery Supply:   lateroinferior part of pons, middle cerebellar peduncle, floccular region, anterior petrosal surface of cerebellar hemisphere ◊  Quite variable course + vascular supply with reciprocal relation between vasc
